key_points: The poem expresses the poet’s desire to live a simple, peaceful life close to nature on Innisfree.
It emphasizes solitude, harmony with nature, and escape from urban life.
The poet imagines building a small cabin, growing his own food, and living near a lake.
Themes include peace, simplicity, natural beauty, and introspection.
The poem creates a calm and meditative mood through imagery of the lake, trees, and bees.
examples: The poet plans to build a cabin and live alone on the island.
The imagery of 'bee-loud glade' highlights the natural sounds and tranquility.
The poem contrasts the peace of Innisfree with the busy life in the city.
exam_tips: Focus on the theme of solitude and peaceful life in nature.
Remember key imagery: cabin, lake, trees, and bees.
Understand the contrast between urban life and natural retreat.
Pay attention to the poet’s feelings of longing and meditation.
Be able to explain the poem’s mood of calm, serenity, and simplicity.
